standard_text: examine career opportunities in each of the five health science career pathways
identify education, licensure, and certification requirements for health science careers
create a personal career plan with consideration of career goals, educational pathways, and employability requirements
demonstrate professionalism including punctuality, appearance, attitude, and respect for diversity
communicate using medical terminology including word roots, prefixes, and suffixes
demonstrate effective verbal and nonverbal communication skills with patients, families, and team members
use accurate and appropriate written communication including documentation standards
identify communication barriers and adaptations for diverse patient populations
identify characteristics of effective leaders in health care
apply team-building and interprofessional collaboration skills
describe roles and scopes of members of the health care team
describe HIPAA privacy and security rules and the protection of patient confidentiality
identify patient rights and the elements of informed consent
recognize scope of practice and licensure requirements for health care roles
apply ethical principles (autonomy, beneficence, non-maleficence, justice) to health care scenarios
demonstrate standard precautions and describe the chain of infection
perform hand hygiene technique according to current guidelines
demonstrate correct selection, donning, and doffing of personal protective equipment
apply proper body mechanics and safe patient handling techniques
respond to fire and emergency situations using established protocols
describe body organization including levels, planes, cavities, and directional terms
identify the major body systems and describe their primary functions
interpret unfamiliar medical terms by analyzing word parts
recognize common medical abbreviations and the Joint Commission do-not-use list
measure and record vital signs within normal ranges for adults
describe the role of electronic health records and basic documentation practices
identify emerging technologies used in health care delivery
describe the U.S. health care delivery system including providers, settings, payers, and regulators
identify components of wellness and the levels of disease prevention
describe cultural competence and humility in health care delivery
